channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Tampa Bay hasn’t been hit directly by a major hurricane since 1921. Milton may be the one
item.description: FORT MYERS BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Florida's Gulf Coast braced Tuesday for the impact of Hurricane Milton's winds and expected massive storm surge, which could bring destruction to areas already reeling from Helene's devastation 12 days ago and still recovering from Ian's wrath two years ago.
item.dc:creator: TERRY SPENCER and HAVEN DALEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Credit card rates hit historic highs as banks raise fees
item.description: The average credit card interest rate reached 21.5% in May, the highest level since the Fed began tracking the data in 1994, according to Bankrate.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court to hear challenge to ghost-gun regulation
item.description: The Supreme Court is hearing a challenge to a Biden administration regulation on ghost guns, the difficult-to-trace weapons with an exponentially increased link to crime in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Here's why experts are so worried about Hurricane Milton
item.description: Residents of the Sunshine State are no strangers to hurricanes, but experts are sounding the alarm over Milton.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KREX 5

item.title: California 7-Eleven clerk brutally beaten in series of 'flash mob' attacks to hit Los Angeles-area stores
item.description: A California 7-Eleven store owner was attacked during a "flash mob" robbery on Sunday. "Flash mob" robberies have been occurring throughout the summer since July.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Singer Cissy Houston, mother of Whitney Houston, dies at 91
item.description: Cissy Houston, the mother of the late Whitney Houston and a two-time Grammy winner who performed alongside superstar musicians like Elvis Presley and Aretha Franklin, has died. She was 91.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Are hurricanes intensifying more rapidly than ever before?
item.description: Rapid intensification is defined by the National Hurricane Center as an increase of at least 30 knots (about 35 mph) in maximum sustained winds within a 24-hour period, which can mean a jump of two categories on the Saffir-Simpson scale.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Artist Nat Ramos draws on cultural roots to shape music career
item.description: Rising artist Nat Ramos shares insight on their musical upbringing, while highlighting the influence of their heritage.
item.dc:creator: Karina F. Garcia, NewsNation

item.title: Florida doctor arrested, accused of running deadly 'pill mill'
item.description: The investigation started after several pharmacists complained about the large amounts of oxycodone being prescribed.
item.dc:creator: Hannah O'Gara, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court takes the bench with ghost guns, a capital case and transgender rights on the docket
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court took the bench again on Monday, ready to hear cases on ghost guns, a death sentence and transgender rights.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Mandatory evacuations begin in Tampa Bay ahead of Milton
item.description: Ahead of Tropical Storm Milton's anticipated landfall as a major hurricane in Florida this week, mandatory evacuations in Tampa Bay have begun.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, NewsNation

item.title: Man falls 200 feet to his death at Zion National Park
item.description: A man fell while canyoneering at Zion National Park on Saturday, resulting in his death, according to the NPS.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, NewsNation

item.title: Major North Carolina newspaper knocks Trump over Helene response 'falsehoods'
item.description: A leading newspaper in North Carolina is blasting former President Donald Trump over what it calls his "falsehoods" about the government response to Hurricane Helene, which devastated large swaths of the state last week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Border town buying drones to deal with migrant emergencies
item.description: The City of Sunland Park is acquiring drones so its fire and police departments can more effectively rescue stranded migrants and hikers on Mount Cristo Rey and individuals struggling in the Rio Grande.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Hurricane Milton strengthens into a Category 5 as Florida prepares for evacuations
item.description: Milton has rapidly strengthened in the Gulf of Mexico, becoming a Category 5 storm Monday on a path toward Florida.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: GOP senator calls Trump special counsel filing 'a temper tantrum from a deranged fanatic'
item.description: Arkansas Sen. Tom Cotton (R) said in a Sunday interview that a recently unsealed filing from special counsel Jack Smith is “a temper tantrum from a deranged fanatic.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: North Carolina first responders exchange vows among chaos in Hurricane Helene aftermath
item.description: A North Carolina first responder couple honored their love for each other with a commitment ceremony on Oct. 2 after Hurricane Helene robbed them of their initial wedding plans.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Stenhouse snaps 65-race losing streak after late crash at Talladega scrambles playoff picture
item.description: Ricky Stenhouse Jr. snapped a 65-race losing streak by winning in overtime at Talladega on Sunday after a late crash collected more than half the field.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hurricane Milton is growing stronger as it blows toward Florida's Tampa Bay region
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — Hurricane Milton quickly intensified Sunday and is on track to become a major hurricane with the Tampa Bay area in its sights, putting Florida on edge and triggering evacuation orders along a coast still reeling from Helene’s devastation.
item.dc:creator: FREIDA FRISARO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Groups suspend use of 'suicide capsule' pending criminal probe of US woman's death
item.description: Swiss police arrested several people following the death of an unidentified 64-year-old woman from the U.S. Midwest who on Sept. 23 became the first person to use the device.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Nibi the 'diva' beaver won't be released, Massachusetts governor decides
item.description: The question of whether a 2-year-old beaver named Nibi can stay with the rescuers she has known since she was a baby or must be released into the wild has been resolved, thanks to the governor of Massachusetts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Many loathe public speakerphone calls, so why do people do it?
item.description: Few things are as universally reviled as a loud phone conversation infringing on your personal space. (Mosquitoes and taxes, aside.) Doing it on loudspeaker is even more incendiary.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: New Jersey sisters battle stage 4 colon cancer together
item.description: They share a lot more than their sister bond. They are both in their late 30s and currently battling stage four colon cancer.
item.dc:creator: Grace Gomez, KRON4

item.title: Does a cumulus cloud really weigh a million pounds?
item.description: Just because something floats doesn't mean it is weightless. A great example is when oil is poured onto water. The oil will float on top of the water, but that doesn't mean it has no weight.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Bacca, Fox 5

item.title: A Texas execution is renewing calls for clemency. It's rarely granted
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — A Texas man set to die this month is at the center of another push for clemency in the U.S., this time backed by several GOP lawmakers and bestselling author John Grisham, who say a father’s 2002 conviction for killing his infant daughter deserves a second look.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN and SUMMER BALLENTINE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ex- Virginia cop who killed shoplifting suspect acquitted of manslaughter, guilty on firearm charge
item.description: FAIRFAX, Va. (AP) — A jury on Friday acquitted a former police officer in Virginia of involuntary manslaughter after he fatally shot a shoplifting suspect outside a busy shopping mall.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BARAKAT and OLIVIA DIAZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge denies an order sought by a Black student who was punished over his hair
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A federal judge on Friday denied a request by a Black high school student in Texas for a court order that the student’s lawyers say would have allowed him to return to his high school without fear of having his previous punishment over his hairstyle resume.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why are there still so many blackouts after major storms?
item.description: The high costs of infrastructure improvements likely have contributed to the blackouts. Experts say more can be done to prevent power outages, but it would require significant investments.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Ohio woman's viral TikTok videos lead prompt search for body in her yard
item.description: Police in Columbus, Ohio have concluded an investigation after a woman posted on TikTok that she might have discovered a body buried in her yard.
item.dc:creator: Katie Millard, Fox 5

item.title: Man charged with helping Idaho inmate escape during a hospital ambush sentenced to life in prison
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — An Idaho man who prosecutors said ambushed and shot correctional officers at a Boise hospital in a plot to help a fellow white supremacist gang member escape from prison was sentenced to life behind bars on Friday.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Satellite shows before-and-after flooding in North Carolina after Helene
item.description: Planet Labs captured and compared satellite images that show the storm's devastating impact on one of the hardest hit cities.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Ullmann, Fox 5

item.title: South Carolina sets Nov. 1 execution as state ramps up use of death chamber
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — South Carolina's high court on Friday set a date of Nov. 1 to put to death a man who killed a store clerk a quarter-century ago, the second of an expected six executions in about six months as the state ramps up its use of capital punishment after a 13-year pause.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Subway rider shot in the head by police files claim accusing officers of recklessly opening fire
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A lawyer for a New York City subway rider who was inadvertently shot in the head by police at a station has filed a legal claim against the city, accusing the officers of showing “carelessness and reckless” disregard for the lives of others when they opened fire on a man holding
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Country music star Garth Brooks accused of sexual assault by makeup artist
item.description: Garth Brooks, the country music superstar who gained fame in the 1990s with several hit songs, has been named in a lawsuit accusing him of raping a hair and makeup artist in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Twin babies who died alongside their mother in Georgia are youngest-known Hurricane Helene victims
item.description: Obie Williams could hear babies crying and branches battering the windows when he answered his daughter’s daily phone call last week as Hurricane Helene tore through her rural Georgia town.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal agencies warn of scams in wake of Helene
item.description: “These criminals take advantage of victims before, during, and after a natural disaster strikes, targeting people when they are most vulnerable, and their methods to commit fraud are wide-ranging,” one U.S. Attorney said.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Voters in 13 states have less than a week to register. When is the deadline in Illinois?
item.description: The 2024 presidential election is fast approaching, and in some states, the voter registration deadline is as soon as this week.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, WGN 9
